headline: Amazon Fires Hit 40-Year Low as Brazil Reverses Devastating Trend
meta_description: Brazil's Amazon burned just 3.1 million hectares in 2025, the smallest area since monitoring began in 1985, marking a sharp reversal from 2024's crisis.
summary_short: Brazil's Amazon experienced its smallest burned area since 1985, with only 3.1 million hectares affected in 2025—down from a devastating 15.8 million in 2024.
body:
Brazil's Amazon rainforest experienced a dramatic recovery in 2025, with burned area plummeting to 3.1 million hectares—the smallest on record since the deforestation monitoring group MapBiomas began tracking fires in 1985. This represents a stunning 80% drop from 2024's catastrophic 15.8 million hectares, when the world's largest rainforest faced its worst fires in over two decades. The turnaround reflects the lowest number of fires recorded in the Amazon in 41 years, offering hope that intensive protection efforts can yield measurable results in one of Earth's most vital ecosystems.

The improvement extends beyond the Amazon alone. Across all of Brazil, approximately 12.7 million hectares burned in 2025, compared to 30 million hectares in 2024—approaching levels not seen since 2007, when records began. Vera Arruda, a researcher at the Amazon Environmental Research Institute and coordinator at MapBiomas, cautioned against premature celebration, noting that "the 31% drop compared to the historical average is positive news, but it does not yet allow us to conclude that this is an established trend." She emphasized that prevention efforts cannot be scaled back.

The gains partly reflect cooler La Niña weather patterns replacing 2024's warmer El Niño conditions, which had intensified fires across the region. However, scientists warn that El Niño could return and extend the dry season, reigniting fire risk. President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who pledged on his first day in office to achieve "zero deforestation in the Amazon," will likely cite these figures as evidence his administration's protection policies are working—a significant boost ahead of his re-election campaign in October.
quick_facts: 3.1 million hectares burned in Amazon in 2025, the lowest since 1985
Lowest number of fires recorded in the Amazon in 41 years
Brazil-wide: 12.7 million hectares burned in 2025 vs. 30 million in 2024
MapBiomas cautions this may not yet signal an established trend
